prompt:
While researching a tornado warning system, a student took the following notes:
• New radar detects rotation earlier.
• Alerts now arrive minutes sooner.
• Earlier warnings save lives.

The student wants to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: By detecting rotation earlier and sending alerts minutes sooner, the new radar gives families precious extra time that saves lives.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts.

choices.text: Thanks to the upgraded system, the warnings now reach people in the storm's path several minutes ahead of before.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The recently installed radar is able to pick up the rotation inside a storm earlier than the older equipment could.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A tornado, a violently spinning column of air, can flatten buildings within seconds of touching down on the ground.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a vertical farm, a student took the following notes:
• The farm grows lettuce in stacked indoor trays.
• It uses 95% less water than a field.
• It produces greens year-round in any weather.

The student wants to emphasize the water savings. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Because it operates entirely indoors, the vertical farm can produce fresh greens during every season of the year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The farm relies on stacked trays of lettuce arranged in tall rows inside a carefully controlled indoor space.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Vertical farming, a method of growing crops in stacked layers, is being tried in cities short on open land.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

choices.text: By growing lettuce in stacked indoor trays, the farm produces year-round greens while using 95% less water than a field.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the water savings.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the water savings; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a city composting program, a student took the following notes:
• Residents sort food scraps into green bins.
• The scraps become compost for public parks.
• The program cut landfill waste by a fifth.

The student wants to report the program's main result. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Since residents began sorting food scraps into green bins, the city has cut the waste sent to its landfill by a fifth.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to report the program's main result.

choices.text: Composting, the natural breakdown of organic matter into rich soil, has long been practiced by home gardeners.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Across the city, residents now carefully sort their leftover food scraps into the green bins set out for collection.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The compost made from the collected scraps is later spread across the soil of the city's many public parks.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to report the program's main result; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a wetland bird census, a student took the following notes:
• Counters logged 84 species in spring.
• That is up from 61 a decade ago.
• Restored marshland likely drove the gain.

The student wants to highlight the increase over time. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: This spring, the volunteer counters carefully logged a total of 84 different bird species across the wetland.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the increase over time as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The gain in the number of species is most likely the result of the marshland that was restored in recent years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the increase over time as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A census, which is a careful count of a population, is repeated regularly so that changes can be tracked over time.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the increase over time as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Spring counts have climbed from 61 species a decade ago to 84 today, a gain likely driven by restored marshland.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the increase over time.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the increase over time; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a rooftop beehive project, a student took the following notes:
• Hives sit atop a downtown office.
• City flowers give bees a long season.
• The bees pollinate nearby park gardens.

The student wants to highlight the benefit to surrounding plants. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: From their hives atop the downtown office, the bees range out to pollinate the flowering gardens of nearby parks.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the benefit to surrounding plants.

choices.text: The beehives that anchor the project are set up on the flat rooftop of a tall downtown office building.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the benefit to surrounding plants as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A bee, an insect that gathers nectar and pollen, lives within a hive alongside thousands of its fellow workers.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the benefit to surrounding plants as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because the surrounding city offers blooms for much of the year, the bees enjoy an unusually long season.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the benefit to surrounding plants as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the benefit to surrounding plants; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a coastal restoration project, a student took the following notes:
• The marsh lost 40% of its area since 1990.
• Volunteers replanted native grasses in 2023.
• Bird sightings rose the following spring.

The student wants to present the project's result. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: After volunteers replanted native grasses, bird sightings in the marsh rose the following spring.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to present the project's result.

choices.text: In 2023, a group of dedicated volunteers who care deeply about the coast decided to replant native grasses in the marsh.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the project's result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Having lost 40% of its area since 1990, the marsh had clearly declined long before volunteers ever arrived to help.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the project's result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The marsh, which had lost much of its area since 1990, is the kind of place where native grasses can be replanted.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the project's result as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to present the project's result; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a citizen science app, a student took the following notes:
• Users photograph wild plants and animals.
• Experts verify each submitted identification.
• The data helped map a rare frog's range.

The student wants to show how the app advanced real research. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Every identification that a user submits through the app is later checked and verified by a qualified expert.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Using the app, ordinary people across the region photograph the wild plants and animals they happen to encounter.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Citizen science, in which volunteers help gather data, lets researchers collect far more observations than usual.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

choices.text: After experts verified the photos that users submitted, the app's data helped scientists map the full range of a rare frog.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to show how the app advanced real research.

explanation: The goal is to show how the app advanced real research; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a bike commuter survey, a student took the following notes:
• Half of riders started during the pandemic.
• Most cite cost savings as their main reason.
• Few plan to return to driving.

The student wants to explain why people took up cycling. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: The survey gathered responses from a broad range of commuters who now travel to work on two wheels each day.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why people took up cycling as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Most riders surveyed took up cycling for the savings, and few of them now plan to go back to driving a car.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain why people took up cycling.

choices.text: According to the survey, roughly half of the city's bike commuters first started riding during the pandemic years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why people took up cycling as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Cycling, an inexpensive and healthy way to get around, has grown more popular in many crowded urban centers.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why people took up cycling as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain why people took up cycling; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a solar farm, a student took the following notes:
• The farm covers 50 acres.
• It powers about 9,000 homes.
• It replaced a closed coal plant.

The student wants to stress the shift from fossil fuels. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Solar farms such as this one generate electricity by capturing sunlight with rows of panels spread across the ground.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the shift from fossil fuels as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Stretching across 50 acres, the solar farm is large enough to supply electricity to roughly 9,000 nearby homes.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the shift from fossil fuels as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Built on the site of a closed coal plant, the 50-acre solar farm now powers about 9,000 homes.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to stress the shift from fossil fuels.

choices.text: The solar farm, which now occupies 50 acres of open land, delivers enough power for about 9,000 households in the area.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the shift from fossil fuels as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to stress the shift from fossil fuels; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a community garden, a student took the following notes:
• The garden has 30 plots.
• Residents grow vegetables and herbs.
• Surplus produce is donated to a food bank.

The student wants to explain how the garden benefits others. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Across its 30 plots, the residents of the neighborhood grow a wide variety of fresh vegetables and fragrant herbs.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the garden benefits others as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The garden is divided into 30 separate plots, each tended by a different resident who grows their own produce.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the garden benefits others as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Beyond its 30 plots of vegetables and herbs, the garden donates surplus produce to a local food bank.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain how the garden benefits others.

choices.text: A food bank, which gathers and distributes donated food, is one of many organizations that serve people in need.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the garden benefits others as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain how the garden benefits others; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching an ancient road discovery, a student took the following notes:
• Archaeologists found a stone road buried under a field.
• The road is about 2,000 years old.
• It connected two former market towns.

The student wants to introduce the discovery to readers. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Archaeologists have uncovered a 2,000-year-old stone road, buried beneath a field, that once linked two market towns.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to introduce the discovery to readers.

choices.text: The road that the archaeologists studied, now roughly 2,000 years old, must have seemed quite modern when it was first built.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Long ago, the stone road connected two market towns whose traders likely carried goods back and forth along its surface.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Archaeologists, the scientists who study the human past, often spend years carefully excavating sites buried under fields.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not introduce the discovery to readers as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to introduce the discovery to readers; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.
